title: Transferring or Selling a Vehicle After a Death
summary: Transferring a vehicle's title after a death has a defined process that varies by state, but generally isn't overly complex.
category: After a Loss III: Estate & Digital
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Transferring or Selling a Vehicle After a Death | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Transferring a vehicle's title after a death has a defined process that varies by state, but generally isn't overly complex.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Requirements vary significantly by state DMV.
A death certificate and proof of inheritance (will or affidavit) are typically required.
Insurance should be updated or transferred before continued use.

paragraphs: Each state's DMV has its own specific process, but most require a certified death certificate along with proof of who inherits the vehicle — either through a will, small estate affidavit, or probate documentation, depending on the estate's size and state rules.

Before continuing to drive or sell the vehicle, confirming that insurance coverage is updated to reflect the new owner is an important, sometimes overlooked step — driving on a lapsed or improperly named policy carries real risk.

nextSteps: Contact your state's DMV to confirm the specific transfer process.
Gather the death certificate and proof of inheritance documentation.
Update insurance before continued use or sale.
disclaimer: General information — your state's DMV is the authoritative source for the specific process required.

sources.name: American Association of Motor Vehicle Administrators
